title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: Agricultural flows from Brazil
Jan-May ‘23: 80% increase in the monthly volume of Brazilian agricultural exports to all destinations
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform, Dry Bulk Flows
The Capesize and Panamax freight rates are showing signs of a revival, suggesting a positive turnaround in the shipping industry. Furthermore, the number of Capesize vessels ballasting in SE Africa has experienced a significant downward trend after maintaining persistently high levels above the annual average for several weeks. This decline in ballasters can potentially reinforce the recent recovery in the sector.

children.summary:
Jan-May ‘23: 80% increase in the monthly volume of Brazilian agricultural exports to all destinations
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-15T130824.965.png | assets/2023-06-15_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-15t130824965_475e35a73f0b.png
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform, Dry Bulk Flows
The Capesize and Panamax freight rates are showing signs of a revival, suggesting a positive turnaround in the shipping industry. Furthermore, the number of Capesize vessels ballasting in SE Africa has experienced a significant downward trend after maintaining persistently high levels above the annual average for several weeks.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT ‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes
section_path: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT ‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes
children.section_path_text: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT ‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes
children.summary:
Market Rates ($/t) Firmer
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-15T131422.119.png | assets/2023-06-15_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-15t131422119_be9cc027f8be.png
The dry freight market began to strengthen, with the Capesize and Panamax segments leading the revival, while weaker momentum was seen in the smaller vessel categories. Capesize vessel freight rates rose to $19.7/tonne, up nearly $ 1/tonne from two weeks ago, but it remains to be seen if the upward sentiment will continue.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas Ballasters (# vessels) Increasing
section_path: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas Ballasters (# vessels) Increasing
children.section_path_text: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas Ballasters (# vessels) Increasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-15T133002.264.png | assets/2023-06-15_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-15t133002264_0dfbb2e3c5c5.png
The number of ballast ships is still on an upward trend, but there are signs of a downward correction in Capesize ships after more than ten weeks of steady increases and a strong upward movement in Handysize. Capesize SE Africa: The current number of ships has now dropped to the annual average of 82, 23 fewer than the previous week.

children.title: SECTION 3/ Demand Summary of Dry Bulk Demand, per Ship Size
section_path: SECTION 3/ Demand Summary of Dry Bulk Demand, per Ship Size
children.section_path_text: SECTION 3/ Demand Summary of Dry Bulk Demand, per Ship Size
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-15T133148.856.png | assets/2023-06-15_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-15t133148856_390b39ab168e.png
The first half of June saw a downward trend in demand for Panamax and Handysize tonnage days, while Capesize vessels gradually regained momentum after bottoming out in week 8. Capesize: Demand growth appears to be one of the strongest points feeding optimism for late June and for a recovery in freight rates.

children.title: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
section_path: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
children.section_path_text: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-15T133306.866.png | assets/2023-06-15_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-06-15t133306866_42d6372e4ebd.png
The first half of June ends with a a sustained upward trend in the number of vessel congestions in the Handysize and Supramax segments. Capesize: The number of vessels is still at a level of almost over 100, with an increase of 20 ships compared to three weeks ago.
